questions.stem: —______ you ______ your homework yet? —Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 【解析】第一空根据yet（已经，常用于疑问句）可知用现在完成时Have...finished；第二空根据an hour ago（一小时前）这一具体过去时间状语，必须用一般过去时finished。A选项第一空错误，yet不与一般过去时连用；C选项第二空错误，ago提示过去时；D选项两空都错。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is playing basketball over there is my classmate Bosco.
questions.explanation: 【解析】先行词The boy指人，且在定语从句中作主语（is playing basketball），故用who。which指物，排除B；whom指人但作宾语，排除C；whose表示所属关系，后需接名词，排除D。口诀：先行词是人作主语用who，作宾语用who/whom，表所属用whose；指物用which/that。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The new library ______ in our school last year.
questions.explanation: 【解析】主语The new library与build是被动关系（图书馆被建），且时间状语last year提示一般过去时，故用一般过去时的被动语态was built。A是主动语态一般现在时；B是主动语态一般过去时；D是被动语态一般现在时，时态不符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: —Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door? —It ______ be him. He has gone to Beijing on business.
questions.explanation: 【解析】根据后句He has gone to Beijing（他去北京了，还没回来）可知，敲门的不可能是他。can't表示否定推测'不可能'；must表示肯定推测'一定是'；might表示可能性较小的推测'也许'；should表示'应该'。本题考查否定推测，选can't。这是你的易错点，注意has gone to表示去了未回。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The word 'sacrificing' in the sentence 'Parents are always sacrificing their own hobbies to spend more time with their children' probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 【解析】根据上下文，父母为了陪孩子而放弃自己的爱好，sacrifice意为'牺牲、放弃'。give up放弃；take up开始从事/占据；look up查阅；put up张贴/举起。这是你的薄弱点，做词义猜测题要结合上下文逻辑，看到'为了...而...'通常是取舍关系。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: What does the prefix 'dis-' mean in words like 'dislike' and 'disagree'?
questions.explanation: 【解析】dis-是常见否定前缀，表示'不、相反'。dislike不喜欢=not like；disagree不同意=not agree。un-也表示否定（unhappy）；re-表示再次（rewrite）；-ful表示充满（careful）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: As a good student, you should ______ your younger sister when your parents are busy.
questions.explanation: 【解析】look after照顾，照看；look for寻找；look up查阅/抬头看；look at看。根据句意'父母忙时照顾妹妹'，选B。注意区分look短语：after跟在后面→照顾；for为了找→寻找；up向上→查阅；at在某点→看。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 【解析】前文说'起初学生觉得难'，后文说'一周后情况改善'，前后是转折关系，用However然而。Therefore因此（因果）；Besides此外（递进）；Instead代替（选择）。完形填空要注意上下文逻辑关系词。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The goal was to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

In the beginning, many students found it hard to change their habits. They used to throw everything into one bin. (8)______, after a week of training, things began to improve. Students learned to separate waste into different groups.

My classmate Tom took the lead. He designed a poster showing how to sort garbage. His creative idea (9)______ by our teacher and won first prize. "Small actions can make a big difference," Tom said.

By the end of the month, we had collected 200 kilos of recyclable materials. The money from selling these items (10)______ to buy books for children in rural areas. Everyone felt proud of our achievement.

This project taught us that protecting the environment is not just a slogan. It requires everyone's (11)______ and daily effort. Now, our "Zero Waste" habit has become part of our school life.

The project was so successful that other schools in our city want to (12)______ our example. We hope more people will join us to make our city greener.

questions.explanation: 【解析】主语His creative idea（他的创意）与praise（表扬）是被动关系，且事件发生在过去（last month），用一般过去时被动语态was praised。A是主动语态过去式；C是主动语态现在时；D是主动语态进行时。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The goal was to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

In the beginning, many students found it hard to change their habits. They used to throw everything into one bin. (8)______, after a week of training, things began to improve. Students learned to separate waste into different groups.

My classmate Tom took the lead. He designed a poster showing how to sort garbage. His creative idea (9)______ by our teacher and won first prize. "Small actions can make a big difference," Tom said.

By the end of the month, we had collected 200 kilos of recyclable materials. The money from selling these items (10)______ to buy books for children in rural areas. Everyone felt proud of our achievement.

This project taught us that protecting the environment is not just a slogan. It requires everyone's (11)______ and daily effort. Now, our "Zero Waste" habit has become part of our school life.

The project was so successful that other schools in our city want to (12)______ our example. We hope more people will join us to make our city greener.

questions.explanation: 【解析】主语The money与use是被动关系（钱被用来...），且动作发生在过去（last month），用一般过去时被动语态was used。be used to do sth被用来做某事。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The goal was to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

In the beginning, many students found it hard to change their habits. They used to throw everything into one bin. (8)______, after a week of training, things began to improve. Students learned to separate waste into different groups.

My classmate Tom took the lead. He designed a poster showing how to sort garbage. His creative idea (9)______ by our teacher and won first prize. "Small actions can make a big difference," Tom said.

By the end of the month, we had collected 200 kilos of recyclable materials. The money from selling these items (10)______ to buy books for children in rural areas. Everyone felt proud of our achievement.

This project taught us that protecting the environment is not just a slogan. It requires everyone's (11)______ and daily effort. Now, our "Zero Waste" habit has become part of our school life.

The project was so successful that other schools in our city want to (12)______ our example. We hope more people will join us to make our city greener.

questions.explanation: 【解析】everyone's（所有人的）是名词所有格，后需接名词participation（参与）。participate是动词；participating是现在分词；participated是过去式。require one's participation需要某人的参与。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:

In the beginning, many students found it hard to change their habits. They used to throw everything into one bin. (8)______, after a week of training, things began to improve. Students learned to separate waste into different groups.

My classmate Tom took the lead. He designed a poster showing how to sort garbage. His creative idea (9)______ by our teacher and won first prize. "Small actions can make a big difference," Tom said.

By the end of the month, we had collected 200 kilos of recyclable materials. The money from selling these items (10)______ to buy books for children in rural areas. Everyone felt proud of our achievement.

This project taught us that protecting the environment is not just a slogan. It requires everyone's (11)______ and daily effort. Now, our "Zero Waste" habit has become part of our school life.

The project was so successful that other schools in our city want to (12)______ our example. We hope more people will join us to make our city greener.

questions.explanation: 【解析】want to do sth想要做某事，to后接动词原形follow。follow one's example学习某人的榜样/效仿某人。

questions.group_passage:

questions.stem: According to the passage, how many hours do teenagers spend on their smartphones daily on average?
questions.explanation: 【解析】细节理解题。根据第一段第二句'A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours daily on their smartphones.'可知是7小时。注意average表示平均数，daily表示每天。做细节题要快速定位关键词，准确找到原文对应句。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Do you know how much time you spend on your phone every day? A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours daily on their smartphones. This has raised concerns among parents and educators.

Dr. Li, a scientist from Shenzhen University, conducted an experiment with 100 middle school students. Half of them were asked to reduce their screen time to 2 hours a day for two weeks, while the other half continued their normal usage.

The results were surprising. The group with limited screen time reported better sleep quality and higher concentration in class. "When I stopped checking my phone every few minutes, I found more time for reading and sports," said one participant.

However, completely banning (禁止) phones isn't the solution. "Technology is not the enemy," Dr. Li explained. "It's about finding a balance. We need to teach students to use phones as tools rather than letting them control our lives."

The study suggests setting specific "phone-free" periods, such as during meals and before bedtime. Schools and families should work together to help teenagers develop healthy digital habits.

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Li's experiment?
options: A. All students hate using smartphones.
B. Reducing screen time has positive effects on students.
C. Smartphones should be banned at all schools.
D. Students don't need technology for learning.
questions.explanation: 【解析】推理判断题。根据第三段'The group with limited screen time reported better sleep quality and higher concentration in class'（减少屏幕时间的组报告睡眠质量更好、课堂注意力更集中）可推断出减少屏幕时间有积极影响。A说所有学生都讨厌手机，过于绝对；C说应该禁止手机，与第四段'completely banning phones isn't the solution'矛盾；D说学生不需要科技，与文意相反。推理题要基于原文信息合理推断，不能过度推理或与原文矛盾。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Do you know how much time you spend on your phone every day? A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours daily on their smartphones. This has raised concerns among parents and educators.

Dr. Li, a scientist from Shenzhen University, conducted an experiment with 100 middle school students. Half of them were asked to reduce their screen time to 2 hours a day for two weeks, while the other half continued their normal usage.

The results were surprising. The group with limited screen time reported better sleep quality and higher concentration in class. "When I stopped checking my phone every few minutes, I found more time for reading and sports," said one participant.

However, completely banning (禁止) phones isn't the solution. "Technology is not the enemy," Dr. Li explained. "It's about finding a balance. We need to teach students to use phones as tools rather than letting them control our lives."

The study suggests setting specific "phone-free" periods, such as during meals and before bedtime. Schools and families should work together to help teenagers develop healthy digital habits.

questions.stem: What does the word 'banning' in Paragraph 4 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 【解析】词义猜测题。根据后句'Technology is not the enemy'（科技不是敌人）和'finding a balance'（寻找平衡）可知，前文说'完全禁止手机不是解决办法'，banning意为禁止=forbidding。A允许；C购买；D制作。做词义猜测题要看上下文逻辑关系，后文解释前文，通过'isn't the solution'和'enemy'可猜出是负面行为即禁止。这是你的薄弱点，要加强练习。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Do you know how much time you spend on your phone every day? A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours daily on their smartphones. This has raised concerns among parents and educators.

Dr. Li, a scientist from Shenzhen University, conducted an experiment with 100 middle school students. Half of them were asked to reduce their screen time to 2 hours a day for two weeks, while the other half continued their normal usage.

The results were surprising. The group with limited screen time reported better sleep quality and higher concentration in class. "When I stopped checking my phone every few minutes, I found more time for reading and sports," said one participant.

However, completely banning (禁止) phones isn't the solution. "Technology is not the enemy," Dr. Li explained. "It's about finding a balance. We need to teach students to use phones as tools rather than letting them control our lives."

The study suggests setting specific "phone-free" periods, such as during meals and before bedtime. Schools and families should work together to help teenagers develop healthy digital habits.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The History of Mobile Phones
B. How to Repair Your Smartphone
C. Finding Balance in Digital Life
D. The Danger of Reading Books
questions.explanation: 【解析】主旨大意题。文章先指出青少年使用手机时间过长的问题，再通过实验证明减少屏幕时间的好处，最后提出'寻找平衡'的解决方案，强调合理使用而非完全禁止。C选项'在数字生活中寻找平衡'最能概括全文。A手机历史，文中未提；B修理手机，偏离主题；D阅读的危险，与文章相反。主旨题要抓住首尾段和每段主题句，注意高频出现的概念（balance, screen time, habits）。这是你的薄弱点，建议多做标题归纳练习。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Do you know how much time you spend on your phone every day? A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours daily on their smartphones. This has raised concerns among parents and educators.

Dr. Li, a scientist from Shenzhen University, conducted an experiment with 100 middle school students. Half of them were asked to reduce their screen time to 2 hours a day for two weeks, while the other half continued their normal usage.

The results were surprising. The group with limited screen time reported better sleep quality and higher concentration in class. "When I stopped checking my phone every few minutes, I found more time for reading and sports," said one participant.

However, completely banning (禁止) phones isn't the solution. "Technology is not the enemy," Dr. Li explained. "It's about finding a balance. We need to teach students to use phones as tools rather than letting them control our lives."

The study suggests setting specific "phone-free" periods, such as during meals and before bedtime. Schools and families should work together to help teenagers develop healthy digital habits.

options: A. However, don't let hobbies take over your study time.
B. As a result, you will get better grades immediately.
C. Planning ahead is very important.
D. In addition, use your spare time wisely.
E. Finally, communicate with your parents about your plans.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选句填空题。后文'First, make a weekly schedule'（首先制定周计划）讲的是具体规划，C选项'提前计划很重要'作为主题句引出下文。注意first提示这是第一条建议，前面需要总起句。做选句填空要看段落结构和连接词，此处是总分结构。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance their studies and hobbies. Here are some tips to help you manage your time better.

(17)______. First, make a weekly schedule. Write down all your homework and exam dates. Then, look for free slots where you can enjoy your hobbies.

(18)______. If you love playing basketball but have a test tomorrow, study first and play later. This way, you can enjoy your hobby without worry.

(19)______. Use short breaks between classes to relax. Even 10 minutes of drawing or listening to music can help you feel refreshed.

(20)______. Tell your family about your schedule so they can support you. Remember, balance is key to a healthy school life.

options: A. However, don't let hobbies take over your study time.
B. As a result, you will get better grades immediately.
C. Planning ahead is very important.
D. In addition, use your spare time wisely.
E. Finally, communicate with your parents about your plans.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选句填空题。后文举例'如果明天有考试，先学习后打球'，强调不能让爱好影响学习，A选项'然而，不要让爱好占据学习时间'符合逻辑。However表示转折，引出与上文'找空闲时间'相对的内容，即要分清主次。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance their studies and hobbies. Here are some tips to help you manage your time better.

(17)______. First, make a weekly schedule. Write down all your homework and exam dates. Then, look for free slots where you can enjoy your hobbies.

(18)______. If you love playing basketball but have a test tomorrow, study first and play later. This way, you can enjoy your hobby without worry.

(19)______. Use short breaks between classes to relax. Even 10 minutes of drawing or listening to music can help you feel refreshed.

(20)______. Tell your family about your schedule so they can support you. Remember, balance is key to a healthy school life.

options: A. However, don't let hobbies take over your study time.
B. As a result, you will get better grades immediately.
C. Planning ahead is very important.
D. In addition, use your spare time wisely.
E. Finally, communicate with your parents about your plans.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选句填空题。后文讲'利用课间10分钟休息'，D选项'此外，明智利用碎片时间'与之对应。In addition表示递进，补充另一个建议。注意spare time与short breaks的对应关系。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance their studies and hobbies. Here are some tips to help you manage your time better.

(17)______. First, make a weekly schedule. Write down all your homework and exam dates. Then, look for free slots where you can enjoy your hobbies.

(18)______. If you love playing basketball but have a test tomorrow, study first and play later. This way, you can enjoy your hobby without worry.

(19)______. Use short breaks between classes to relax. Even 10 minutes of drawing or listening to music can help you feel refreshed.

(20)______. Tell your family about your schedule so they can support you. Remember, balance is key to a healthy school life.

options: A. However, don't let hobbies take over your study time.
B. As a result, you will get better grades immediately.
C. Planning ahead is very important.
D. In addition, use your spare time wisely.
E. Finally, communicate with your parents about your plans.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选句填空题。后文'Tell your family'（告诉家人）与E选项'与父母沟通你的计划'完全对应。Finally表示最后一点建议，与前面的First形成呼应。做选句填空要注意连接词（first, however, in addition, finally）和代词指代（your family=your parents）。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance their studies and hobbies. Here are some tips to help you manage your time better.
